business.title: SBI Q4 FY26 Results Live Updates: State Bank of India among key companies to announce Q4 results today. Check profit, NII and other estimates
Axis Bank, HDFC Bank, other bank stocks drop up to 2%, Nifty Bank falls 440 points. What lies ahead?
business.summary: Banking stocks dragged the Nifty Bank index lower by nearly 440 points amid weakness in heavyweight lenders like HDFC Bank and Axis Bank. Broader market pressure from rising oil prices and FII outflows weighed on sentiment, while analysts flagged key support and resistance levels for the index in the near term.

business.title: Shakti Pumps shares tumble 7% as Q4 profit drops 65% YoY
business.summary: Shakti Pumps saw revenue climb significantly. However, costs rose sharply, impacting profits. Earnings per share dropped considerably. Despite this, the board proposed a dividend. The company's stock has fallen over the past year. Institutional investors have reduced their holdings, indicating a cautious market sentiment.

business.title: Jefferies blames HDFC Bank controversy for impacting banking sector valuations
business.summary: Jefferies said the leadership uncertainty at HDFC Bank has become a major overhang for the banking sector, dragging benchmark valuations despite strong fundamentals. The brokerage believes clarity on the bank’s management succession, along with easing West Asia tensions, could trigger a recovery in sector earnings and valuations.

business.title: HFCL shares drop 2% after skyrocketing 92% in one month. Time to buy or better to wait?
business.summary: HFCL shares saw a 2% dip after a significant 92% surge in a month which prompted profit booking. Despite this, the stock has rallied nearly 147% year-to-date. Analysts at Geojit maintain a 'Buy' rating with a target of Rs 150, citing a strong order book, defense sector expansion, and favorable long-term fiber demand outlook.

business.title: AI trade turns Adani’s energy stocks into India proxy play
business.summary: Adani Group's significant investment in green-powered data centers is driving substantial gains in its energy units, positioning them as key AI infrastructure plays in India. The conglomerate's ambitious plans to develop AI-ready data centers, coupled with India's surging electricity demand, have propelled Adani's energy stocks to outperform the market.

business.title: 2 Indian nationals part of crew of luxury cruise ship that reported hantavirus outbreak
business.summary: WHO said five of the eight suspected hantavirus cases had been confirmed

business.title: No AI, poor returns drive Dalal Street investors to foreign markets
business.summary: Indian investors are increasingly shifting money overseas in search of diversification amid weaker domestic market returns, persistent foreign fund outflows, and a record-low rupee. Overseas investments in equities and debt rose 60% year-on-year to over $2.2 billion in the 11 months through February.

business.title: Bottom 50% of Nagaland households receive only 18% of total income: Report
business.summary: The state recorded a Gini coefficient of 0.46, a measure commonly used for income inequality, where 0 represents perfect equality and 1 indicates extreme inequality

business.title: Global perceptions of US fall below Russia under Trump, survey finds
business.summary: The Denmark-based Alliance of Democracies Foundation, which commissioned the survey, noted that the US was frequently identified as the greatest threat to the world, following Russia and Israel

business.title: Odisha attracts Rs 48,330 crore investment potential at Gujarat investors’ meet

business.title: Indian Air Force, DRDO conduct maiden flight-trial of Tactical Advanced Range Augmentation weapon
business.summary: The DRDO-developed system was successfully tested off the Odisha coast with IAF support

business.title: AI boom masks rising global energy risks, warns David Roche
business.summary: Market veterans warn investors may be underestimating the long-term economic fallout from the Middle East energy crisis. While AI spending and US economic resilience fuel optimism, falling oil reserves and tightening supplies could lead to shortages and a significant global GDP contraction.  Rising shipping costs and insurance premiums further exacerbate these concerns.

business.title: Shell’s Profit Surge: How Iran Tensions Powered Q1 Earnings
business.summary: Shell reported its strongest quarterly profit in nearly two years, driven by gains in refining and energy trading amid Iran-related geopolitical tensions. While earnings beat expectations and shareholder payouts increased, concerns over declining production and sustainability of trading gains weighed on investor sentiment following the results announcement.

business.title: BSE share price target rises up to Rs 4,570 after Q4 results. What Jefferies, Motilal Oswal & others are saying?
business.summary: BSE shares dipped despite a strong March quarter performance, with net profit soaring 61% YoY to Rs 797 crore and revenue jumping 85% to Rs 1,564 crore. Analysts remain divided, with Nuvama maintaining a 'Buy' rating and Jefferies a 'Hold', citing varying growth prospects and risks.

business.title: FMCG recovery real but modest, banking margins face pressure, power sector demand unstoppable: Anand Tandon
business.summary: India's earnings season reveals a steady, albeit unspectacular, recovery. FMCG sees rural demand lift, but growth remains modest. PSU banks boast clean balance sheets, yet face quiet pressure on net interest margins. Power and EMS sectors have strong demand, but margin sustainability hinges on raw material costs and rising competition.

business.title: Fresh US-Iran tensions drag India bonds lower ahead of new 10-year debt sale
business.summary: Indian government bonds experienced a decline today. This happened as oil prices increased due to renewed conflict between the U.S. and Iran. Investors are also watching a new 10-year bond auction. Higher oil prices could impact India's inflation and economy. The market awaits the auction results for further direction.

business.title: Bitcoin slips under $80K amid Iran-U.S. uncertainty despite $1 billion ETF inflows; profit booking rises
business.summary: Bitcoin has dipped below the $80,000 mark. This pullback follows a strong rally and is influenced by rising geopolitical tensions. Investors are booking profits, and leveraged positions are being liquidated. Despite this, spot Bitcoin ETFs continue to see significant inflows, indicating ongoing institutional interest. The market is navigating a consolidation phase, with attention shifting to economic data.

business.title: US Stock Market: US Treasury Bond Yields near 5% again as inflation fears shake Wall Street
business.summary: Wall Street is abuzz as investors debate if US 30-year Treasury bond yields will remain above 5 percent. Rising oil prices and a robust US economy are fueling this concern. This could mean higher borrowing costs for everyone. Markets are also reassessing the US Federal Reserve's next moves. The situation demands close attention as it impacts global financial markets.

business.title: Sonata Software shares rally 10% as Q4 net profit rises 21% despite revenue contraction; firm announces dividend
business.summary: Sonata Software shares surged nearly 10% after the company reported a 21% rise in Q4 FY26 net profit, despite a decline in revenue. Strong EBITDA growth and deal wins supported sentiment, while the firm announced a final dividend. Segment performance remained steady, with gains in both international and domestic businesses.
